summary: Pieter follow-up on the proposal sent April 3. He explained his standard practice of calling 48 hours after sending any quote because the in-app activity tracking is unreliable. Lisa appreciated the proactive call and said she'd reviewed the proposal but had a question about the project-tracking module pricing. Next step: revised quote with the SMB tier confirmed.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Pieter
transcript.text: Hi Lisa, this is Pieter — I sent the proposal Wednesday.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Ja, ik heb 'm gezien. We waren druk maar ik heb 'm doorgelezen.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Pieter
transcript.text: Good — I always call about 48 hours after sending any quote. The tracking in our system isn't reliable enough for me to trust, so I just call.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: That's nice actually. Most vendors just assume silence means no.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Pieter
transcript.text: Right. So before I waste your time — did the proposal answer your questions, or are there gaps?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: One thing — the project-tracking module pricing. The line item said 'starts at €X per user per month' but I have 12 users and the math didn't work out.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Pieter
transcript.text: Probably an issue with the volume tier. Let me revise and send a corrected quote.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Thanks. Send 'm en ik bel je terug.
